majors.count_method: counted unique bachelor's-level program titles from the official undergraduate programs page
titles: Real Estate and the Built Environment
majors.notes: counted unique bachelor's-level program titles from the official undergraduate programs page; deduped BA/BS variants and excluded minor-only, dual-degree, and bachelor's-completion entries: https://www.du.edu/academics/undergraduate-programs

admissions.testing_policy: The University of Denver is test-optional; submitting SAT or ACT scores is the applicant’s choice, and scores are considered with the rest of the application if submitted.
admissions.gpa_policy: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.
admissions.course_rigor: DU says it considers grades and coursework and likes to see that applicants challenged themselves and achieved success in subjects like English, math, social studies, science, and foreign language.
admissions.recommendations: DU's general how-to-apply guidance lists additional letters of recommendation as optional supplemental materials, though some programs or applicant types may have specific recommendation requirements.
admissions.essays: DU requires one application essay through the Common Application; students may choose any current-year Common App prompt for that required essay.

evidence.claim: The University promotes a healthy and safe educational, professional, and residential community where drugs and alcohol do not interfere with individual performance, personal success, public safety or the integrity of the learning environment. Compliance with the federal requirements under the Drug Free Schools and Communities Act and Drug Free Workplace is available here.
evidence.source_excerpt: The University promotes a healthy and safe educational, professional, and residential community where drugs and alcohol do not interfere with individual performance, personal success, public safety or the integrity of the learning environment. Compliance with the federal requirements under the Drug Free Schools and Communities Act and Drug Free Workplace is available here.

evidence.claim: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.
evidence.source_excerpt: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 5 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'University of Denver' (legacyId=1095) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 10 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, then ranked by pageviews.

evidence.claim: Recorded 62 unique bachelor's-level program titles from DU's official undergraduate programs page.

evidence.claim: DU says it considers grades and coursework and likes to see that applicants challenged themselves and achieved success in subjects like English, math, social studies, science, and foreign language.
evidence.source_excerpt: We consider your grades and coursework... We like to see you’ve challenged yourself and achieved success in subjects like English, math, social studies, science and foreign language.

evidence.claim: DU's general how-to-apply guidance lists additional letters of recommendation as optional supplemental materials, though some programs or applicant types may have specific recommendation requirements.
evidence.source_excerpt: Application essay (included in the Common App) Additional letters of recommendation (optional) Official ACT or SAT scores (optional)

evidence.claim: DU requires one application essay through the Common Application; students may choose any current-year Common App prompt for that required essay.
evidence.source_excerpt: The application includes an essay requirement... Students are free to choose from any of the current year's Common Application prompts for their one required essay.

evidence.claim: The University of Denver is test-optional; submitting SAT or ACT scores is the applicant’s choice, and scores are considered with the rest of the application if submitted.
evidence.source_excerpt: However, we are a test-optional university, and submitting test scores is your choice. If you choose to submit your SAT and/or ACT scores, they will be considered along with your other application materials for both admission and merit scholarships.

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (62 titles).
Phase 5 auto-enrichment completed using official-school web crawl heuristics; manual spot-checking is still recommended for edge cases.
GPA policy was normalized from absence of an explicit minimum-GPA statement on the cited official pages.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

University of Denver majors and admissions fields were manually repaired from official undergraduate programs and application-checklist pages after the prior crawl captured navigation-heavy majors text and left essay/course-rigor gaps.
University of Denver testing policy was manually repaired from the official admission standards page after the auto-crawl captured drug/alcohol compliance text.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'University of Denver' (legacyId=1095) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Wikipedia list/category candidates ranked by trailing-365-day Wikipedia pageviews.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, then ranked by pageviews.
